steps.title: Find the shown leg length
steps.content: The segment shown has endpoints $(-6,4)$ and $(3,10)$. Its horizontal change is $9$ and its vertical change is $6$, so its length is $$\sqrt{9^2+6^2}=\sqrt{117}=3\sqrt{13}.$$

steps.title: Use the triangle area formula
steps.content: Let the other leg be $x$. The area is $36\sqrt{13}$, so $$36\sqrt{13}=\frac{1}{2}(3\sqrt{13})x.$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $2$: $$72\sqrt{13}=3\sqrt{13}x.$$ Divide by $3\sqrt{13}$ to get $$x=24.$$ B is correct.
